name: Thai Kaeng Khua Saparot (Prawn and Pineapple Curry)
recipeInstructions:
The pineapple adds a touch of tart sweetness to this dish. It has a rich creamy texture and a red coloration that complements the shrimp.
If you want to be fancy, serve it in pineapple skin bowls.
1. Peel, devein, and behead the shrimp, leaving only the small tail shells on.
2. Drop two tomatoes into boiling water for about a minute, then remove to cold water. Peel, quarter, discard the seed pulp, and chop the flesh. You want 2 tablespoons of chopped tomatoes.
3. In a little oil in a medium hot wok, briefly saute the garlic until aromatic, then remove it and set aside.
4. Cook the curry paste in the same wok to develop the aroma.
5. Add half the coconut milk, fish sauce, chopped tomatoes, pineapple, lime juice, and palm sugar, stirring to combine fully.
6. Add the prawns and cook until they turn slightly pink and opaque.
7. Add the remainder of the coconut milk, lime leaves, and slivered chili. Return the sauteed garlic and basil to the pan.
8. Transfer to a serving dish and serve with steamed jasmine rice.
